verses.text: Haggai and Zechariah prophesy, and Zerubbabel and Jeshua rise to build again, the prophets of God helping them. A fresh word from God revives the stalled work. When preaching stirs the conscience, hands long idle take up the work afresh.

Haggai and Zechariah prophesy, and Zerubbabel and Jeshua rise to build again, the prophets of God helping them. A fresh word from God revives the stalled work. When preaching stirs the conscience, hands long idle take up the work afresh.

Tatnai the governor demands to know who authorized the building, 'but the eye of their God was upon the elders,' so the work was not stopped. God watches over His own. While men question and threaten, His unseen care keeps the work moving on.

Tatnai the governor demands to know who authorized the building, 'but the eye of their God was upon the elders,' so the work was not stopped. God watches over His own. While men question and threaten, His unseen care keeps the work moving on.

Tatnai the governor demands to know who authorized the building, 'but the eye of their God was upon the elders,' so the work was not stopped. God watches over His own. While men question and threaten, His unseen care keeps the work moving on.

Tatnai writes to Darius, describing the great stones and timber and the swift progress, and asks for the builders' names. The matter is referred upward honestly. God can give His people favour even with the officials sent to investigate them.

Tatnai writes to Darius, describing the great stones and timber and the swift progress, and asks for the builders' names. The matter is referred upward honestly. God can give His people favour even with the officials sent to investigate them.

The elders answer plainly: 'We are the servants of the God of heaven and earth,' rebuilding what was destroyed for the fathers' sin, by the decree of Cyrus. They confess their God and their history without shame. A clear, humble testimony is the best defence of God's work.

The elders answer plainly: 'We are the servants of the God of heaven and earth,' rebuilding what was destroyed for the fathers' sin, by the decree of Cyrus. They confess their God and their history without shame. A clear, humble testimony is the best defence of God's work.

The elders answer plainly: 'We are the servants of the God of heaven and earth,' rebuilding what was destroyed for the fathers' sin, by the decree of Cyrus. They confess their God and their history without shame. A clear, humble testimony is the best defence of God's work.

They recount how Cyrus restored the temple vessels and appointed Sheshbazzar to lay the foundation, and the work has continued since. They rest their case on a king's own decree. Truth, simply told, needs no embellishment to stand.

They recount how Cyrus restored the temple vessels and appointed Sheshbazzar to lay the foundation, and the work has continued since. They rest their case on a king's own decree. Truth, simply told, needs no embellishment to stand.
They invite the king to search his archives whether Cyrus truly issued such a decree. They are willing to be tested by the record. A good cause does not fear investigation but welcomes it.
